query:
question about crt-029: Two things are happening together:                                                                                     
   
  1. The source selection may not be rotating. If select_source_candidates orders isolated entries by ID ascending, ticks
   1 and 2 are selecting the same ~65 real candidates (after skipping no-embedding entries). Those candidates' pairs were
   already tried in tick 1 — the 6 that passed were written, the rest were below threshold. Tick 2 re-scores the same    
  pairs and gets the same 0 results. The tick has no memory that a source candidate's neighbors were already
  NLI-evaluated and rejected.

  2. The corpus at 0.5/0.7 thresholds may only have 6 easy edges. The decision/pattern/procedure entries in this topic   
  cluster may genuinely not have many high-entailment pairings beyond what was already captured. Lesson-learned entries
  especially narrate specific events and don't entail each other in the NLI sense.                                       
                                                                  
  This is a design gap worth filing as a follow-up. The tick needs either rotation (shuffle/randomize source selection so
   it doesn't retry the same exhausted candidates) or a "pair attempted" record to skip re-scoring pairs that were
  already below threshold.                                                                                               
                                                                  
  Immediate practical question: Does source candidate selection randomize or is it deterministic? Check                  
  select_source_candidates in nli_detection_tick.rs — if it's ORDER BY id or similar without randomization, that confirms
   the stuck state. If it randomizes, tick 3 should show different candidates and possibly new edges.

entries.title: Tick passes over full entry set: bound, shuffle, and embedding-filter source candidates independently of NLI pair cap
ADR-002 crt-023: NLI Entailment Score Replaces rerank_score for Search Re-ranking Sort
Tick source-candidate selection with stable ordering stalls after first window is exhausted
NLI neutral-zone score is the detection signal for cross-category institutional memory edges
